verification_gate: Owner-approved decision assigns every relevant data class exactly once, preserves justified current capabilities, identifies derived and transient copies, defines directional synchronization, durability, recovery, and the transition path across redb, PostgreSQL+RuVector, and nu_plugin CodeDB.

gate_result.synchronization_direction: forward-only redb -> envctl(embed) -> PostgreSQL; no dual-write
gate_result.capabilities_preserved_via: BlobStore SHA-256 parity contract (codedb_core::store::BlobStore, codedb_store_pg::PgStore, blobstore_parity.rs) — verified per-class cutover, no data loss
gate_result.upgrades_not_downgrades: honored by verified-cutover migration mechanism, not by freezing redb as durable; moving redb to its intended transient role is building-to-spec
gate_result.prior_brief_mistake: inverted authority direction; treated CodeDB's transitional durable-redb usage as a capability to preserve indefinitely and mislabeled migration-to-spec a downgrade
open_dependencies: live RuVector-in-PostgreSQL extension activation (semantic-class full cutover)
live blobstore_parity.rs green run against disposable PostgreSQL (durable-class cutover proof)

proof_summary: STORE-001 is decided per the Architecture Blueprint (foundational source of truth): PostgreSQL + RuVector is the single canonical durable owner of relational + semantic macro-state; redb is the transient high-frequency tier (buffer/scratchpad/WAL/dedup-cache/geometry engine); AgentDB (.rvf) owns per-agent non-authoritative cognition; envctl is the forward-only bridge and projection engine; nu_plugin CodeDB is the parser/ingestion path. Every macro-state class is assigned exactly once with a single durable writer (PostgreSQL), forward-only synchronization (redb -> envctl -> PostgreSQL), per-class recovery, and identified transient/derived copies. The decision corrects the prior store_001_decision_brief.md, which inverted the intended authority direction by treating CodeDB's transitional durable-on-redb blobs as a permanent capability and mislabeling the migration-to-spec a downgrade. Per the blueprint's tiered hierarchy and the Architecture Context Matrix (PostgreSQL+RuVector = 'Canonical state', redb = 'Temporary/derived state'), redb's intended role is transient; building toward that is an upgrade. Upgrades-not-downgrades is preserved by the migration mechanism: the already-implemented BlobStore SHA-256 parity contract (PgStore + blobstore_parity.rs) makes each per-class cutover a verified swap under one content identity, executed only after a green live PostgreSQL parity run — never by assumption. No product execution or store mutation was performed; this records the class-by-class owner decision that unblocks PGAUTH-001..006, POSTGRES-001, FOUNDATION-001, and LIFEOS-002.
